headline: From afar, American students in France feel the shockwaves of Trump
description: As academic freedom declines in the US, some young people are choosing France to pursue master's and doctoral degrees. Yet, between delayed scholarships, intrusive questions and stereotypes, their experience remains shaken by the latest developments in their country.

description: Gert Vercauteren, director of Belgium's Coordination Unit for Threat Analysis, assesses the risk of terrorist attacks, 10 years after the 2016 Brussels attacks.

headline: Danish PM Mette Frederiksen's Social Democrats lead election but fall short of majority
description: With all votes counted, the left-wing bloc was credited with 84 seats in the 179-seat parliament and the right with 77. Frederikson, a favorite going into elections, has been praised for her leadership after fending off US President Trump's repeated demands to annex Greenland, a Danish autonomous territory.

headline: Five affordable vegetarian restaurants in the Paris region
description: Craving a quinoa arancini, an Asian-inspired salad or a coconut dessert? Here is our selection of places in Paris and the suburbs where you can enjoy quality vegetarian food for under €20.

description: The Socialists and right-wing Les Républicains can boast of successes in the second round of France's municipal elections on March 22. But both parties remain deeply weakened by the lack of a clear direction and the fragmentation of the political landscape, writes Le Monde columnist Françoise Fressoz.

headline: France's aircraft carrier located in real time by Le Monde through Strava data
description: As the Charles de Gaulle and its strike group approach the Middle East, Le Monde identified a French sailor using the Strava fitness application in the Mediterranean Sea. This security flaw remains unaddressed despite our previous revelations.
